description: Implement the field-of-view (FOV) and vision cone system. The player has a directional 90-degree vision cone; tiles outside the cone are fully black. Previously seen tiles render as dim 'memory' tiles. This is the signature visual mechanic that creates tension.
acceptance_criteria: Given the player is facing a direction, when the scene renders, then only tiles within a 90-degree forward cone (plus a small peripheral radius) are fully visible.
Tiles outside the vision cone but within the player's room render at 30% brightness in desaturated gray (memory tiles).
Tiles that have never been seen render as fully black.
When the player changes facing direction, the vision cone rotates to match.
Line-of-sight is blocked by wall tiles — the player cannot see through walls even within the cone.
Light sources in powered rooms override the cone: tiles in a lit, powered room are visible regardless of player facing.
spec_section: 5.1 Facing and Vision, A.3.1 Vision Cone & Darkness System
notes: Spec calls this 'THE signature visual mechanic.' Implement raycasting or shadowcasting for LOS. Canvas globalAlpha + fillRect overlay for darkness. Memory tile data stored in a 2D boolean array.
